title: GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction 50 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic education program for Latina women working towards a GED, including coursework in English Language Development (ELD), mathematics, grammar, parenting, and an 18-week seminar focused on conflict resolution (ESPERE). The Early Childhood Education program provides preschool and child care for participants' children. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Spanish-speaking women and their children, ages 6 weeks-5 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Classes are held 5 days per week for a full year Email (503) 992-0078 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANTFARM AXIS LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth education center with academic and youth support services. Provides 13 computers to create, facilitate learning, and access to information. Assisting young people with completion of high school, establishment of educational goals, and movement towards college and/or work readiness are core services offered. Community members offer support through tutoring, mentoring, community volunteering, job-skills training, supported employment, and assisting young people with problem solving and critical thinking skills. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 668-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AT:PROJECT ANTI-TRAFFICKING ALLIANCE OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Anti-Trafficking Project and Cannabis Workers Program provides human and labor trafficking hotline services, including safety planning, risk assessments, hygiene items, clothing, food, mental health, substance abuse treatment referrals, applying for public assistance benefits, and document replacement. In addition, the program offers assistance with GED, resume building, transportation to interviews, and referrals to local services for job training and career development. Case management and medical accompaniment services are also available. All services are confidential. Eligibility: Anti-Trafficking Project: Individuals who are experiencing or survivors of sex or labor trafficking or exploitation Youth who have experience Commercial Sexual Exploitation of Children (CSEC). Cannabis Workers Program: Individuals who have worked in the marijuana industry who have experienced wage loss, unsafe living and working conditions, violence, labor exploitation, or human trafficking. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 647-0658 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers community college education including university transfer degrees, career and technical education certificates and degrees, adult basic skills and GED preparation, college readiness, workforce training, continuing and community education, and lifelong learning opportunities. The Adult Basic Education (ABE) program is for individuals with or without a high school diploma or GED to help prepare for college-level coursework, advance in their careers, or achieve personal milestones by strengthening essential reading, writing, and math. Campuses are located in Pendleton (main campus), Hermiston, Milton-Freewater, Baker City, Boardman, La Grande, and John Day. 2411 NW Carden Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must be age 16 and not enrolled in school. Students under age 18 must have a release from school district in which they reside before attempting to pass the GED test. Hours: Schedule varies Email (541) 276-1260 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Campuses are located in Bend, Redmond, Madras, and Prineville. 2600 NW College Way Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 383-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY CENTRO DE PROSPERIDAD HILLSBORO ADULT BASIC EDUCATION, JOB SEARCH, AND BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers educational programs to individuals who are seeking to improve employability. Programs include GED tutoring and computer literacy. Also assists clients in attaining job readiness through job application assistance, interview preparation, resume building and locating internship and on the job training (OJT) opportunities. Business development services are offered at no cost to those who wish to start their own business and to existing small business owners who wish to improve business practices. 265 SE Oak Street Suite A Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Email (503) 368-9895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE 2ND CHANCE SHELTER PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services such as limited assistance obtaining Oregon ID, mailing addresses, GED tutoring, and essential supplies as available (such as toothpaste, shampoo, soap, and deodorant). Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! MCMINNVILLE SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 117 NE 5th Street Suite H McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 883-9768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! NEWBERG SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 100 E 5th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. education and professional development classes, and English as a Second Language classes. Campus locations in Astoria and Seaside. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 338-2411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE THE DALLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 400 E Scenic Drive Building 2, Room 2.035 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-6pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 506-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM ALBANY YOUTH HOUSE YOUTH HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Also provides a paid work experiance opptunity. Work experiance pays minimum wage (examples of work experiance include community garden, MakerSpace, city library, maintenance or local business) 130 SE 4th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by program with a focus on youth ages 16-24. Hours: No standard hours (541) 758-2631 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM ALBANY YOUTH HOUSE YOUTH BUILD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth employment program with a focus on assisting community partners with a variety of construction, land restoration, and essential maintenance projects. Also helps participants work toward their GED or high school diplomas. 130 SE 4th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm (541) 758-2631 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM CORVALLIS YOUTH HOUSE AND GARDENS YOUTH HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Hours: No standard hours (541) 758-2605 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM HENRY HILL POLK COUNTY YOUTH PROGRAM POLK GED AND CAREER PATHWAYS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Also provides a paid work experiance opptunity. Work experiance pays minimum wage and is a total of 200 hours (examples of work experiance include community garden, MakerSpace, city library, maintenance or local business) 750 5th Street S Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 and not enrolled in highschool or college Hours: Phone Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 704-7820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center providing a space to socialize, have coffee and snacks, receive mail, phone charging, employment support, information from local health and social organizations, and counseling. Provides assistance with acquiring clothing and shoes; personal hygiene items; medical and dental fees/services; and can assist with transportation needs. Also offers navigation services such as applying for SNAP; completing, obtaining, or replacing official documents like driver's license and birth certificates; access to health providers; housing assistance wait lists; and obtaining a GED. 530 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income individuals and families; individuals with disabilities Hours: Basic needs: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Social Services support: Monday-Friday noon-2pm by appointment Email (458) 233-5327 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 214 W 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL JOB CONNECTION CAREER CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides training classes in Microsoft Office. Instructors offer Microsoft Office curriculum and other learning resources to help all community members reach their career goals. -Microsoft Office classes and certificates -Computer skills classes and certificates (Computer Navigation, Email Navigation, Internet Navigation) -Workplace Preparedness classes and certificates -National Career Readiness NCRC classes and certificates -GED preparation support 1943 SE Sixth Avenue Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by location (503) 238-6100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 7390 S 6th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 882-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LA PINE PARKS AND RECREATION ADULT PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides education, recreation, health, and business programs. 16405 First Street La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 536-2223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transfer degrees, 40+ career technical programs, and a variety of non-credit courses. Academic counseling and Career Information System (CIS), which lists job occupations of interest are offered as well. Provides career planning, academic, and personal advising, adult basic education, various instructional programs, disability services, telecourses, and distance learning. 4000 E 30th Avenue Building 24 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 463-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers classes to help native and non-native speakers improve their math, reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills. Classes include Adult Basic Education, GED preparation, Spanish GED preparation, skill improvement, and English as a Second Language. The main campus is in Albany with centers in Corvallis, Lebanon, and Sweet Home. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Varies by location, visit website or call for information on current term offerings Email (541) 917-4710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one tutoring. Students learn to read, write, learn English as a second language, improve basic math skills, and prepare for the citizenship test. Assists to improve job skills, pass the written driver's exam, fill out job applications and other forms, read medicine labels and signs, and learn to use a computer in an educational capacity. Offers a specialized program designed to help those with the learning disability dyslexia learn to read, write, and spell. Also helps students qualify for enrollment at Rogue Community College (RCC) and assists them in preparing for their GED in language arts, math, science, and social studies. 132 NE B Street Suite 23 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 472-0355 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS SCHOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational services for homeless, runaway, and at-risk of becoming homeless youth. Classes for credit include middle and high school basics of language arts, math, sciences, and social studies. Students will also be offered GED preparation/tutoring. Students will receive support with resumes, employment applications, forms, and bills. In some situations, class times can be arranged to fit individual needs. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless, runaway, and at-risk of becoming homeless youth ages 11-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 302-2551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RIVERFRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER RIVER FRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accredited alternative school offering educational and vocational training. Services include comprehensive career and basic skill assessment, computer skills instruction, work readiness and job search assistance, and job training programs. Assists with transportation, job search clothing, child care, and GED testing. 1666 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: At-risk and out-of-school youth ages 14-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 302-2554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-VALLEY LITERACY CENTER ADULT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides literacy services. Offers computer basics, adult English classes for English Language Learners, workplace English and Spanish Language Literacy, preparation for GED, United States citizenship, and Certified Nursing Assistants certifications. 1850 45th Avenue NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-8pm Email (503) 463-1488 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and ESL classes. 26000 SE Stark Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary (503) 491-6422 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY GED HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers online resources, study materials, and databases to prepare for the General Educational Development (GED) test. Provides wraparound services, including one-on-one tutoring, test fees, and bus tickets. Eligibility: Age 18 and older, not currently in high school, and have not graduated from high school Hours: Varies by location Email (503) 577-9984 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and information on how to access college. Offers one-on-one support from teachers and tutors. Participates currently enrolled in case management with New Avenues for Youth may be eligible for an educational scholarship who are attending career technical education or postsecondary education!. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are at-risk or experiencing housing instability Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Email (503) 358-9142 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Main campus is in Newport with locations in Lincoln City and Waldport. 400 SE College Way Newport, OR 97366 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 867-8501 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE HIGH SCHOOL OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accredited, tuition free, alternative high school serving youth. Offers a 22 week residential phase and a 12 month post-residential phase, where academically at-risk students can earn 8-10 high school credits and/or their GED or high school diploma upon completion. 23861 Dodds Road Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: -Ages 15-18 (must be 16 by graduation) -Legal resident of the US and State of Oregon -Academically deficient (behind in high school credits), at-risk of not graduating or a high school drop-out -Free from felony convictions and no open violations for adult status offenses -Physically and mentally capable of participating Hours: Office staff available Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, OYCP operates 24/7 Email (541) 317-9623 223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER EDUCATION AND EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and testing, GED/high school credit recovery and tutoring that is Portland Public School accredited, work readiness training, job search help, resume help, college preparation, and help with post-secondary education and training. "Urban Ed" is a drop-in, student-centered alternative school for youth who have dropped out or are at risk of dropping out of school. Work experiences are available at the Virginia Woof (VW) Dog Daycare and Youth Job Training Program. Child care is also available for established clients. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are low-income, homeless, or at risk of homelessness Hours: who do not have a high school diploma. Also, offers a high school completion program where students earn a high school diploma and college credits that can be applied towards an associate degree, certificate program, or transferred to a four-year college or university. Provides English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) test preparation. 2305 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 16-20 in need of an alternative education placement. Requires school district approval. Hours: Hours vary Email (971) 722-6293 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE SYLVANIA CAMPUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language. 12000 SW 49th Avenue Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-4pm (971) 722-6111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND YOUTHBUILDERS YOUTHBUILD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an alternative high school and job training program for young people who are ready to earn a GED or high school diploma and launch a career. Provides education, skills, structure, and GED test instruction. Program includes academic instruction in small classes leading to a GED or diploma; hands-on job training in technology or construction; industry-recognized certifications, preparation for college, apprenticeship, and career-path employment, career coaching, and wrap-around support; free bus passes; opportunities to earn an AmeriCorps education award and small financial bonuses for achievement; and multi-year support and placement assistance after graduation. Students attend school full-time for roughly 12 months and split their time between the classroom and PYB jobsites. In PYB's Technology track, students learn coding and programming and build and upgrade computers as a community service. PYB's Construction track is a certified pre-apprenticeship program in which students build and renovate homes for low-income families. PYB helps students in both tracks obtain employment and post-secondary education or training. 4816 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Low-income individuals ages 17-24. Applicants ages 17-20 must live within a partnering school district. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 286-9350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including pre-college education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language(ESL) classes. Campuses are located in Tillamook, Rockaway Beach, and Cloverdale. 4301 Third Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm (503) 842-8222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. The main campus is located in Ontario, Oregon, with a center in Caldwell, Idaho. 650 College Boulevard Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 881-8822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Umpqua Community College's Adult Basic Skills Development program helps adults who want to get their GED, improve their reading and math skills, prepare for college or a career, and learn English as a Second Language (ESL). Offers classes for people who have earned their diploma or GED and want to improve their basic education skills. Classes are held in various areas, including Roseburg, Glide, and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. ESL classes are held in Roseburg and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. Free tutoring program, school-to-career program, and several computer labs also availble to students. 1140 Umpqua College Road Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 440-4600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tutoring and adult literacy program that offers one-on-one and small group instruction in reading, writing, GED preparation, and English as a Second Language (ESL) courses. 736 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and adults who identify as having low literacy skills Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 672-6336 Get Directions Visit Website More Details USAHELLO Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for integration by providing online resources and information about United States culture, daily life, education, health, jobs, laws, and further immigration explainers. Also offers online citizenship courses, GED test preparation courses, and a database of local services. Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees or immigrants Hours: Not applicable Email (213) 254-5822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY COOPERATIVE LIBRARY SERVICES BRAINFUSE HELPNOW Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides study and practice tests for AP exams, SAT, American College Testing (ACT), General Educational Development (GED), Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery (ASVAB), and US Citizenship. Also provides assistance with writing assessments and provides tutoring for math, reading, writing, science, and social studies.
